description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical stone base and a pitched, red-tiled wooden roof supported by two sturdy wooden beams. The well features a central opening with a visible water surface, and a wooden crank mechanism attached to a horizontal rod for lowering and raising a bucket. A wooden bucket is suspended over the well, and a small metal pail is attached to one side. The well sits on a patch of mixed sand and stone paving, suggesting it is designed for outdoor use. The structure is robust, with the stonework forming a protective barrier around the water, and the roof providing shelter from debris and weather.
description: A cylindrical stone water well with a red-tiled wooden roof, crank mechanism, and bucket, set on a sand and stone base.
description_view_0: The well is seen from the side, highlighting the stone base, wooden support beam, and a small metal pail attached to the left.
description_view_1: The front view shows the crank handle, bucket suspended over the well, and the full width of the red-tiled roof.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the stone base, wooden support, and the metal pail on the right.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the back of the crank mechanism and the roof structure, with the bucket visible over the well opening.
attribute_well_cover_design: No full cover; open central well with a pitched red-tiled wooden roof overhead for protection.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Yes, a wooden crank and horizontal rod mechanism with a suspended wooden bucket and a side metal pail.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Cylindrical stone wall approximately 1 meter high encircling the well opening, with a wooden roof supported by beams.
